channel.title: Using the API on Spotfire® Mods
channel.description: Recent content in Using the API on Spotfire® Mods

item.title: API overview
item.description:
The API overview is an alternative perspective of the API, compared to the more classic API documentation.

The visualization is a simplified view of the API. While the Mod API relies heavily on asynchronous behavior via Promises, the visualization only displays the interfaces defined in the spotfire-api.d.ts file. There are some advanced types available in the declaration file that are not displayed in the hierarchy visualization. The advanced types describe some of the more intricate functionality in the API.

item.title: Visualization mods API documentation
item.description:
The visualiztion mods API documentation lists all interfaces, type aliases, and functions in the visualization mods API. It contains a short introduction and describes how to use them. From each interface you can also find a direct link to the API overview to get a hierarchical perspective.

Older versions

The latest API version is backwards compatible with older 1.x release. All new functionality is marked by a version tag.

Note: There will be a runtime error if a new API feature is used without incrementing the mod manifest’s apiVersion property.

item.title: Mod data views
item.description:
Defining a data view

A mod can define one or more aggregated data view definitions.

The data view is declared in the mod manifest. Its content is specified as a number of axes and an optional color axis. The Spotfire UI will provide controls to set actual columns/expressions used to build the data view in runtime.

In the simplest form, the data view definition part of a mod manifest could look like this.

item.title: Visualization mod manifest schema
item.description:
<iframe id="schema-frame" width="100%" onload="resizeIFrameToFitContent()" src="/spotfire-mods/visualization-mods-schema/index.html"  frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen" allow="geolocation *; microphone *; camera *; midi *; encrypted-media *"></iframe>
<script type="application/javascript">

    function resizeIFrameToFitContent( ) {
        let frame = document.getElementById("schema-frame");
        frame.height = frame.contentWindow.document.body.scrollHeight;
    }
    window.addEventListener("resize",resizeIFrameToFitContent);
</script>

item.title: Working with mod readers
item.description:
The Spotfire Mod API exposes a set of Readables, such as the DataView and properties. These can be consumed by a Reader in order to read their values and subscribe to changes.

mod.createReader(mod.property("myProperty"), mod.windowSize());

Introduction

The aim of a reader is to simplify the synchronization of reading related values. An example is a visualization that depends on the window’s size and the dataview. When one of the two changes, the visualization needs to be redrawn. The reader fetches new values and keeps unchanged values in memory.
